differences.justification: If ToolProviderResult were extended and the subclass already declared a constructor with this same signature at lesser visibility, this change would be a breaking change. ToolProviderResult is not expected to be extended, so this is acceptable.
@Internal removed intentionally: AiServiceTool is now part of the public API and can be constructed and passed to AiServices directly by users (e.g. via AiServices.tools(List<AiServiceTool>)).

CompensableToolExecution is @Internal framework plumbing that was widened from private to public only so the streaming publisher (in a different package) can accumulate compensable executions and pass them back to ToolService.compensateOnCancellationAsync(...). It is a record (implicitly final), so the subclass-with-lesser-visibility-constructor concern behind this check cannot arise, and it is not part of the user-facing API.
CompensableToolExecution is public only for internal cross-package use (see the visibilityIncreased entry above); marking it @Internal documents that it is not user-facing API.
